response: The internet began as a US Department of Defense project in the 1960s, with ARPANET sending its first message in 1969. The adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 and Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 laid the foundation for the modern web, which became accessible to ordinary users with the Mosaic browser in 1993. After the dot-com boom and crash (late 1990s–2001), survivors like Amazon and Google became major companies, while social media (mid-2000s), the iPhone (2007), and cloud computing (AWS in 2006) drove further transformation. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins virtually all aspects of modern life.
